Job Title: Member of the Board of Advisors
Location: City Of Peterborough, England, United Kingdom
Text: Congratulations, you will smash it!

Wow. I rarely (if ever) I find a post that stops me to make me want to comment. This has stopped me in my tracks as it deeply resonates with me. From all my years in L&D I’ve stood on the side lines and seen trainers that “shout the loudest” “control the room” blah blah and had people think that facilitating learning is an easy 
skill based purely on “presentation skills”. I’ve been asked to deliver “train the trainer” as though it’s something that people can just be trained on. True learning facilitation is not about presenting, it’s not about being qualified (CIPD accredited etc) it’s about being authentic, bringing learned life experiences from the people in the room and storytelling then connecting the learning theories to inspire others. Its a humbled skill that takes years of practice to master that’s so often overlooked. I felt this post deeply, thank you.
